mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity prices lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month trend by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates tightening supply-side capacity and rising corporate output, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 2% quarterly annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where inflation exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when payroll growth falls below 100k while unemployment drops.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which systematically produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an imminent supply-side contraction and recession risk, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets rally in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
